findings.title: US 10Y Treasury yield drops to 4.29% on U.S.-Iran ceasefire
findings.summary: Bond yields fell globally following the U.S.-Iran ceasefire announcement, with the 10-year Treasury yield declining to 4.29% as geopolitical risk eased. This reflects reassessment of central bank policy expectations amid lower oil prices. Matters for macro as it signals temporary relief in inflation pass-through risks.
findings.claim_excerpt: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield declined to around 4.29%, while the 2-year yield closed near 3.79%.
findings.signal_type_basis: Episodic geopolitical relief driving yield moves, noise unless sustained 3+ weeks.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Temporary easing of risk premiums; monitor for reacceleration if oil rebounds.
findings.confidence_basis: Direct market data from financial press with consistent yield reporting.

sources_cited.excerpt: Sharp increase in yields recently, but context of bond moves.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Two sources confirm yield decline tied to ceasefire; Edward Jones provides precise levels.

cross_monitor_relevance.scem: Energy price relief eases commodity stress

findings.review_reason: Assess if yield drop sustains beyond geopolitical noise

findings.title: WTI crude plunges 15% to $96/bbl on ceasefire news
findings.summary: Oil prices sharply declined with WTI at $96 per barrel after U.S.-Iran ceasefire eased supply fears. This counters prior Middle East conflict-driven surges. Key for macro as it tempers near-term inflation but exposes tariff-driven energy input ambiguities.
findings.claim_excerpt: Oil prices were sharply lower, with WTI crude down roughly 15% and closing around $96 per barrel.
findings.signal_type_basis: Event-specific reversal from conflict escalation, not sustained trend.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Relief rally; tariff protocol flags net ambiguous exposure for energy.
findings.confidence_basis: Precise price move corroborated across sources.

sources_cited.excerpt: Crude oil prices soared on Middle East conflict, now reversing.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Dual confirmation of price action tied to same event.

findings.episodic_reason: Direct response to U.S.-Iran ceasefire announcement

cross_monitor_relevance.erm: Sharp energy price swing impacts AI capex costs

findings.review_reason: Track if oil stabilizes or rebounds on tariff/energy dynamics

findings.title: China 10Y bond yield falls amid global yield rise
findings.summary: Unlike US/EU/Japan, China's 10Y yield declined since Middle East conflict start, reflecting high domestic savings and investor appeal. Signals resilience amid tariff tensions. Matters as counterpoint to tariff-driven inflation fears elsewhere.
findings.claim_excerpt: China, where the yield on the 10-year bond has fallen modestly since the conflict began.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term divergence in EM bond attractiveness under tariff regime.
findings.preliminary_assessment: China debt safe-haven bid strengthens amid tariff retaliation risks.
findings.confidence_basis: Deloitte analysis with comparative global context.

sources_cited.excerpt: China 10Y yield fallen modestly, exception to global rise.
Global bond yields moved lower post-ceasefire.

search_queries_run: China bond yields April 2026 Middle East
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Sources highlight China exception to global trend.

cross_monitor_relevance.scem: EM bond resilience amid commodity stress

findings.review_reason: Monitor China yield as tariff retaliation proxy

findings.title: US services PMI falls to 49.8 on prices and uncertainty
findings.summary: US services PMI dropped to 49.8, first contraction since 2023, citing rising prices, uncertainty, Middle East war, and tariffs. Impacts tech/services sectors heavily. Signals cyclical strain under tariff shock.
findings.claim_excerpt: PMI for the US services industry fell from 51.7 to 49.8... buckling under rising prices and uncertainty, tariffs.
findings.signal_type_basis: PMI export orders divergence upgraded to CC under active tariff protocol.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Tariff uncertainty amplifying cyclical slowdown in services/tech.
findings.confidence_basis: S&P Global via Deloitte with explicit tariff link.

sources_cited.excerpt: US services PMI 49.8, tariffs exacerbate concerns.
Markets monitor inflation amid energy surge.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Primary source quotes S&P; secondary ties to macro.

cross_monitor_relevance.erm: Services contraction hits AI capex momentum

findings.title: Eurozone inflation accelerates, ECB rate hike priced
findings.summary: Eurozone inflation sped up, with markets pricing ECB benchmark hikes 1-2 times by end-2026. Driven by core pressures amid global yields rise. Elevates ESA decoupling risks under tariff coercion.
findings.claim_excerpt: Eurozone inflation accelerates... ECB will raise benchmark rate one or two times before end 2026.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term policy divergence in tariff environment.
findings.preliminary_assessment: ECB tightening widens US-EU policy gap, active coercion phase.
findings.confidence_basis: Single primary source but futures market implied.

sources_cited.excerpt: ECB rate hikes priced on inflation acceleration.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: One strong source; needs central bank confirmation.

cross_monitor_relevance.esa: ECB hikes signal European fiscal autonomy push

findings.title: US core PCE dips to 3.0% despite energy surge
findings.summary: Core PCE fell to 3.0%, above Fed 2% target, with housing inflation at 3.1% easing services. Energy surge expected to reaccelerate inflation. Temporary relief but tariff/energy risks loom.
findings.claim_excerpt: Core PCE... edged down to 3.0%, still above Fed's 2% target.
findings.signal_type_basis: Monthly data point amid volatile energy, not regime shift.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Sticky but easing core; watch oil feed-through.
findings.episodic_reason: Tied to recent energy price surge
findings.review_reason: Inflation path critical for Fed expectations

findings.title: Dollar drops 1% on ceasefire, EM relief
findings.summary: USD fell nearly 1% vs developed currencies post-ceasefire as geo tensions eased. Provides EM equities breathing room amid tariff pressures. TS move but flags embedded regime dollar strength.
findings.claim_excerpt: Easing geopolitical tensions also weighed on the dollar, down nearly 1%.
findings.signal_type_basis: Episodic geo relief; tariff false positive discipline applies.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Not new tightening; expected risk-off reversal.
findings.confidence_basis: Market move directly tied to event.

sources_cited.excerpt: Financial markets react to Middle East news.

cross_monitor_relevance.scem: EM currency relief from dollar ease

findings.review_reason: Apply false positive check on dollar moves

below_threshold.title: Housing inflation eases to 3.1%
below_threshold.reason_excluded: Lagged real estate signal, monitor quarterly per protocol

tariff_escalation_status.rung_basis: Enacted tariffs with active retaliation by China/EU per protocol activation; no new WTO filings in window
